iwh_id: Malik meriwayatkan to me dari Musa ibn Maysara bahwa dia mendengar a seorang laki-laki ask Said ibn al-Musayyab, "Aku adalah a seorang laki-laki who sells untuk seorang utang." Said said, "Jangan sell kecuali for what you take to your unta." Malik spoke about a seseorang who bought goods from a seorang laki-laki provided bahwa he provide him dengan those goods by a specific date, salah satu in time untuk seorang market in which he hoped for their saleability, atau to fulfil a need di time he stipulated. Then the seller failed him about the date, dan the buyer wanted to return those goods kepada seller. Malik berkata, "The buyer cannot do that, dan the jual beli is binding on him. If the seller does bring the goods sebelum the completion dari term, the buyer cannot be forced to take them." Malik spoke about a seseorang who bought makanan dan measured it. Then some one came to him to buy it dan dia memberitahunya bahwa dia telah measured it for himself dan taken it in full. The new buyer wanted to trust him dan accept his measure. Malik berkata, "Whatever is sold in this way for cash tidak memiliki harm in it namun whatever is sold in this way on delayed terms is disapproved of hingga the new buyer measures it out for himself. The jual beli dengan delayed terms is disapproved of karena it leads to riba dan itu adalah feared bahwa it will be circulated in this way tanpa weight atau measure. If the terms are delayed itu adalah disapproved of dan ada no disagreement about bahwa dengan us." Malik berkata, "One should tidak buy a utang owed by a seorang laki-laki whether present atau absent, tanpa the confirmation dari one who owes the utang, nor should one buy a utang owed kepada seorang seorang laki-laki by a dead seseorang even jika one knows what the deceased seorang laki-laki has left. That is karena to buy bahwa is an uncertain transaction dan one does tidak know whether the transaction will be completed atau tidak completed." He said, "The explanation of what is disapproved of in pembelian a utang owed by someone absent atau dead, is bahwa itu tidak known what unknown debtor may be connected kepada dead seseorang. If the dead seseorang is liable for another utang, the harga which the buyer gave on strength dari utang may become worthless." Malik berkata, "There is another fault in bahwa as well. He is pembelian something which is tidak guaranteed for him, dan so jika the deal is tidak completed, what dia membayar becomes worthless. This is an uncertain transaction dan itu adalah tidak good." Malik berkata, "One distinguishes antara a seorang laki-laki who is hanya penjualan what he actually has dan a seorang laki-laki who is being paid in advance for something which is tidak yet in his possession. The seorang laki-laki advancing the money brings his gold which he intends to buy with. The seller says, 'This is 10 dinar. What do you want me to buy for you dengan it?' It is as jika dia menjual 10 dinar cash for 15 dinar to be paid later. Karena of this, itu adalah disapproved of. It is something leading to riba dan fraud

ar: وَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، قَالَ لاَ تَبِيعُوا الذَّهَبَ بِالذَّهَبِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالْوَرِقِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالذَّهَبِ أَحَدُهُمَا غَائِبٌ وَالآخَرُ نَاجِزٌ وَإِنِ اسْتَنْظَرَكَ إِلَى أَنْ يَلِجَ بَيْتَهُ فَلاَ تُنْظِرْهُ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمُ الرَّمَاءَ وَالرَّمَاءُ هُوَ الرِّبَا ‏.‏
ar_clean: وَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، قَالَ لاَ تَبِيعُوا الذَّهَبَ بِالذَّهَبِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالْوَرِقِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالذَّهَبِ أَحَدُهُمَا غَائِبٌ وَالآخَرُ نَاجِزٌ وَإِنِ اسْتَنْظَرَكَ إِلَى أَنْ يَلِجَ بَيْتَهُ فَلاَ تُنْظِرْهُ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمُ الرَّمَاءَ وَالرَّمَاءُ هُوَ الرِّبَا .
isnad_ar: وَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْ
matn_ar: نِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، قَالَ لاَ تَبِيعُوا الذَّهَبَ بِالذَّهَبِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالْوَرِقِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالذَّهَبِ أَحَدُهُمَا غَائِبٌ وَالآخَرُ نَاجِزٌ وَإِنِ اسْتَنْظَرَكَ إِلَى أَنْ يَلِجَ بَيْتَهُ فَلاَ تُنْظِرْهُ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمُ الرَّمَاءَ وَالرَّمَاءُ هُوَ الرِّبَا .
matn_ar_clean: ن عمر، أن عمر بن الخطاب، قال لا تبيعوا الذهب بالذهب إلا مثلا بمثل ولا تشفوا بعضها على بعض ولا تبيعوا الورق بالورق إلا مثلا بمثل ولا تشفوا بعضها على بعض ولا تبيعوا الورق بالذهب أحدهما غائب والآخر ناجز وإن استنظرك إلى أن يلج بيته فلا تنظره إني أخاف عليكم الرماء والرماء هو الربا .
sunnah_ref_en: Yahya related to me from Malik from Nafi from Abdullah ibn Umar that Umar ibn al-Khattab said, "Do not sell gold for gold except like for like, and do not increase one part over another part. Do not sell silver for silver except like for like, and do not increase one part over another part. Do not sell silver for gold, one of them at hand and the other to be given later. If someone seeks to delay paying you until he has been to his house, do not leave him. I fear rama for you." Rama is usury
iwh_en: Yahya related to me from Malik from Nafi from Abdullah ibn Umar that Umar ibn al-Khattab said, "Do not sell gold for gold except like for like, and do not increase one part over another part. Do not sell silver for silver except like for like, and do not increase one part over another part. Do not sell silver for gold, one of them at hand and the other to be given later. If someone seeks to delay paying you until he has been to his house, do not leave him. I fear rama for you." Rama is usury
iwh_id: Yahya meriwayatkan kepadaku dari Malik dari Nafi dari Abdullah ibn Umar bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab said, "Jangan sell gold for gold kecuali like for like, dan jangan increase one part over another part. Jangan sell silver for silver kecuali like for like, dan jangan increase one part over another part. Jangan sell silver for gold, one of them at hand dan the other to be given later. If someone seeks to delay paying you hingga he has been to his house, jangan leave him. I fear rama for you." Rama is riba

ar: حَدَّثَنِي يَحْيَى، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم قَالَ ‏ "‏ أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ بَاعَ مَتَاعًا فَأَفْلَسَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ مِنْهُ وَلَمْ يَقْبِضِ الَّذِي بَاعَهُ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ شَيْئًا فَوَجَدَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ وَإِنْ مَاتَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ فَصَاحِبُ الْمَتَاعِ فِيهِ أُسْوَةُ الْغُرَمَاءِ ‏"‏ ‏.‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنِي يَحْيَى، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم قَالَ  " أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ بَاعَ مَتَاعًا فَأَفْلَسَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ مِنْهُ وَلَمْ يَقْبِضِ الَّذِي بَاعَهُ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ شَيْئًا فَوَجَدَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ وَإِنْ مَاتَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ فَصَاحِبُ الْمَتَاعِ فِيهِ أُسْوَةُ الْغُرَمَاءِ " .
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنِي يَحْيَى، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَارِث
matn_ar: ِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم قَالَ " أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ بَاعَ مَتَاعًا فَأَفْلَسَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ مِنْهُ وَلَمْ يَقْبِضِ الَّذِي بَاعَهُ مِنْ ثَمَنِهِ شَيْئًا فَوَجَدَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ وَإِنْ مَاتَ الَّذِي ابْتَاعَهُ فَصَاحِبُ الْمَتَاعِ فِيهِ أُسْوَةُ الْغُرَمَاءِ " .
matn_ar_clean: بن هشام، أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال " أيما رجل باع متاعا فأفلس الذي ابتاعه منه ولم يقبض الذي باعه من ثمنه شيئا فوجده بعينه فهو أحق به وإن مات الذي ابتاعه فصاحب المتاع فيه أسوة الغرماء " .
sunnah_ref_en: Malik said there was no harm if a man who sold some drapery and excluded some garments by their markings, stipulated that he chose the marked ones from that. If he did not stipulate that he would choose from them when he made the exclusion, I think that he is partner in the number of drapery goods which were purchased from him. That is because two garments can be alike in marking and be greatly different in price. Malik said, "The way of doing things among us is that there is no harm in partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, and revocation when dealing with food and other things, whether or not possession was taken, when the transaction is with cash, and there is no profit, loss, or deferment of price in it. If profit or loss or deferment of price from one of the two enters any of these transactions, it becomes sale which is made halal by what makes sale halal, and made haram by what makes sale haram, and it is not partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, or revocation." Malik spoke about some one who bought drapery goods or slaves, and the sale was concluded, then a man asked him to be his partner and he agreed and the new partner paid the whole price to the seller and then something happened to the goods which removed them from their possession. Malik said, "The new partner takes the price from the original partner and the original partner demands from the seller the whole price unless the original partner stipulated on the new partner during the sale and before the transaction with the seller was completed that the seller was responsible to him. If the transaction has ended and the seller has gone, the pre-condition of the original partner is void, and he has the responsibility." Malik spoke about a man who asked another man to buy certain goods to share between them, and he wanted the other man to pay for him and he would sell the goods for the other man. Malik said, "That is not good. When he says, 'Pay for me and I will sell it for you,' it becomes a loan which he makes to him in order that he sell it for him and if those goods are destroyed, or pass, the man who paid the price will demand from his partner what he put in for him. This is part of the advance which brings in profit." Malik said, "If a man buys goods, and they are settled for him, and then a man says to him, 'Share half of these goods with me, and I will sell them all for you,' that is halal, there is no harm in it. The explanation of that is that this is a new sale and he sells him half of the goods provided that he sells the whole lot." Yahya related to me from Malik from Ibn Shihab from Abu Bakr ibn Abd ar-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham that the Messenger of Allah, may Allah bless him and grant him peace, said, "Whenever a man sells wares and then the buyer becomes bankrupt and the seller has not taken any of the price and he finds some of his property intact with the buyer, he is more entitled to it than anyone else. If the buyer dies, then the seller is the same as other creditors with respect to it
iwh_en: Malik said there was no harm if a man who sold some drapery and excluded some garments by their markings, stipulated that he chose the marked ones from that. If he did not stipulate that he would choose from them when he made the exclusion, I think that he is partner in the number of drapery goods which were purchased from him. That is because two garments can be alike in marking and be greatly different in price. Malik said, "The way of doing things among us is that there is no harm in partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, and revocation when dealing with food and other things, whether or not possession was taken, when the transaction is with cash, and there is no profit, loss, or deferment of price in it. If profit or loss or deferment of price from one of the two enters any of these transactions, it becomes sale which is made halal by what makes sale halal, and made haram by what makes sale haram, and it is not partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, or revocation." Malik spoke about some one who bought drapery goods or slaves, and the sale was concluded, then a man asked him to be his partner and he agreed and the new partner paid the whole price to the seller and then something happened to the goods which removed them from their possession. Malik said, "The new partner takes the price from the original partner and the original partner demands from the seller the whole price unless the original partner stipulated on the new partner during the sale and before the transaction with the seller was completed that the seller was responsible to him. If the transaction has ended and the seller has gone, the pre-condition of the original partner is void, and he has the responsibility." Malik spoke about a man who asked another man to buy certain goods to share between them, and he wanted the other man to pay for him and he would sell the goods for the other man. Malik said, "That is not good. When he says, 'Pay for me and I will sell it for you,' it becomes a loan which he makes to him in order that he sell it for him and if those goods are destroyed, or pass, the man who paid the price will demand from his partner what he put in for him. This is part of the advance which brings in profit." Malik said, "If a man buys goods, and they are settled for him, and then a man says to him, 'Share half of these goods with me, and I will sell them all for you,' that is halal, there is no harm in it. The explanation of that is that this is a new sale and he sells him half of the goods provided that he sells the whole lot." Yahya related to me from Malik from Ibn Shihab from Abu Bakr ibn Abd ar-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, may Allah bless him and grant him peace, said, "Whenever a man sells wares and then the buyer becomes bankrupt and the seller has not taken any of the price and he finds some of his property intact with the buyer, he is more entitled to it than anyone else. If the buyer dies, then the seller is the same as other creditors with respect to it
iwh_id: Malik berkata ada no harm jika a seorang laki-laki who sold some drapery dan excluded some garments by their markings, stipulated bahwa he chose the marked ones dari that. If dia melakukan tidak stipulate bahwa dia choose dari them ketika dia melakukan the exclusion, I think bahwa dia adalah partner dalam number of drapery goods which were purchased dari him. That is karena two garments can be alike in marking dan be greatly different in harga. Malik berkata, "The way of doing things di antara us is bahwa ada no harm in partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, dan revocation ketika dealing dengan makanan dan other things, whether atau tidak possession was taken, ketika the transaction is dengan cash, dan ada no profit, loss, atau deferment of harga in it. If profit atau loss atau deferment of harga dari one dari two enters any of these transactions, it becomes jual beli which is made halal by what makes jual beli halal, dan made haram by what makes jual beli haram, dan itu adalah tidak partnership, transferring responsibility to an agent, atau revocation." Malik spoke about some one who bought drapery goods atau hamba-hamba sahaya, dan the jual beli was concluded, kemudian a seorang laki-laki asked him to be his partner dan he agreed dan the new partner paid the whole harga kepada seller dan kemudian something happened kepada goods which removed them dari their possession. Malik berkata, "The new partner takes the harga dari the original partner dan the original partner demands from the seller the whole harga kecuali jika the original partner stipulated on the new partner selama the jual beli dan sebelum the transaction dengan the seller was completed bahwa the seller was responsible to him. If the transaction has ended dan the seller has gone, the pre-condition dari original partner is void, dan he has the responsibility." Malik spoke about a seorang laki-laki who asked another seorang laki-laki to buy certain goods to share antara them, dan he wanted the other seorang laki-laki to pay for him dan dia sell the goods untuk other seorang laki-laki. Malik berkata, "That is tidak good. When he says, 'Pay for me dan I will sell it for you,' it becomes a pinjaman which he makes to him in order bahwa he sell it for him dan jika those goods are destroyed, atau pass, the seorang laki-laki who paid the harga will demand dari his partner what he put in for him. This is part dari advance which brings in profit." Malik berkata, "If a seorang laki-laki buys goods, dan mereka adalah settled for him, dan kemudian a seorang laki-laki says to him, 'Share half of these goods dengan me, dan I will sell them all for you,' bahwa is halal, ada no harm in it. The explanation of bahwa is bahwa this is a new jual beli dan he sells him half dari goods provided bahwa he sells the whole lot." Yahya meriwayatkan to me dari Malik from Ibn Shihab from Abu Bakr ibn Abd ar-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whenever a seorang laki-laki sells wares dan kemudian the buyer becomes bankrupt dan the seller has tidak taken any dari harga dan he finds some of his property intact dengan the buyer, dia adalah more entitled to it than anyone else. If the buyer dies, kemudian the seller is the same as other creditors berkenaan dengan it

ar: وَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، قَالَ لاَ تَبِيعُوا الذَّهَبَ بِالذَّهَبِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا الْوَرِقَ بِالْوَرِقِ إِلاَّ مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ وَلاَ تُشِفُّوا بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ وَلاَ تَبِيعُوا شَيْئًا مِنْهَا غَائِبًا بِنَاجِزٍ ‏.‏ وَإِنِ اسْتَنْظَرَكَ إِلَى أَنْ يَلِجَ بَيْتَهُ فَلاَ تُنْظِرْهُ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمُ الرَّمَاءَ وَالرَّمَاءُ هُوَ الرِّبَا ‏.‏
